title: invAIriant self-audit — the framework as its own first subject
scope: The invAIriant framework at db64b5d, audited as a software artifact: docs, lens library, schemas, prompts, templates, skill, cli, examples, CI. In scope: internal consistency, boundaries, and whether the framework upholds its own rules. Out of scope: the subjective quality of individual lens *content*, and the fictional example project (conveyor).
inputs_reviewed: internal markdown link integrity: 139 relative links checked, 0 broken
CI runs (Validate Framework, green on db64b5d)
participants: fable-agent (single-agent run: lens passes, evidence verification, severity, synthesis)

lens_scores.verdict: Skill / schema / CLI boundaries are explicit and upheld in code: cli/invairiant.py runs no lens, produces no finding, assigns no score (docs/cli.md boundary is real, not just stated).
Docs stay synchronized across a large surface (139/139 internal links resolve; CHANGELOG maintained). One debt item: config lens ids are not cross-checked against the library (INV-001).
One coherent story after repositioning: primary skill, secondary protocol layer, helper CLI; the anti-overengineering canon (4-6 lenses) is stated and practiced.
Negative space is explicit and enforced: the 'CLI must not audit' constraint is a stated non-goal and true in cli/invairiant.py.
Docs and CLI are operable (argparse --help, _die messages carry context). Gap: a typo'd config lens id fails late (at audit time) instead of at validate-config time (INV-001).
Small, controlled surface: collect-evidence resolves adapters through a fixed registry and runs list-form subprocesses (no shell); no secrets in the repo; the authorship hook is local-only.

findings.claim: Nothing cross-checks that a config's mandatory_lenses name lenses that actually exist; a typo passes validation but has no lens file, so the audit silently cannot run that lens.

evidence.description: mandatory_lenses items are constrained only by a kebab-case pattern, not by existence in the lens library
neither scripts/validate_framework.py nor cli/invairiant.py validate-config cross-references mandatory_lenses (or critical_lenses) against lenses/*/*.md; a typo like 'mcconel' passes validate-config

findings.risk: A config can reference a dead lens id and pass every check; the omission surfaces only mid-audit, weakening the tooling's role as a safety net around the agentic audit.
findings.recommendation: Add a referential-integrity check (config mandatory_lenses/critical_lenses ⊆ known lens ids) to validate_framework.py and to `invairiant validate-config`, sourcing the id set from lenses/*/*.md.

verification.method: read the schema block; grepped scripts/ and cli/ for any mandatory_lenses existence check — none found

findings.claim: The example report JSON references findings CNV-043 and CNV-044 in its lens verdicts and executive summary but never defines them in findings[]; only CNV-041/042 exist there, while the prose example defines CNV-041..044.

evidence.description: lens_scores verdicts cite CNV-044 (line 29) and CNV-043 (line 32), neither present in findings[]
the prose report defines CNV-041 through CNV-044 as full findings; the JSON's findings[] holds only CNV-041 and CNV-042

findings.risk: A reader diffing the two example artifacts sees dangling finding ids; as the canonical machine-readable sample it should be internally complete. Low impact — example fixtures only.
findings.recommendation: Either add CNV-043/CNV-044 as full findings in the JSON to match the prose, or reword the lens verdicts/summary to not cite ids the JSON does not define.

verification.method: parsed findings[] ids ([CNV-041, CNV-042]); grepped CNV-043/044 (lines 29, 32, 82); cross-read example-audit.md

observations.text: Coverage asymmetry: scripts/validate_framework.py validates example-findings.json against the finding schema but not example-report.json against the audit-report schema; the report schema is only exercised by the CLI smoke test in CI. Not a defect (CI covers it), but the validator and the CLI check overlapping-yet-different subsets.
observations.follow_up: optionally add a report check to validate_framework.py, or document the split of duties.

observations.text: Good-state: internal link integrity is clean — 139/139 relative markdown links resolve at db64b5d.
Good-state: no origin-project jargon leaks into the generic framework; the origin is referenced only generically (README origins note, lenses/domain/network-persistence.md), by design.

hypotheses.text: Security concern investigated: could a hostile invairiant.config.yml make `invairiant collect-evidence` run arbitrary commands?
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Refuted — adapters resolve only through the fixed _ADAPTERS registry in cli/invairiant.py; unknown names are skipped, and subprocess.run uses list form (shell=False). The config selects which known tools run, not what command.

summary.executive_summary: The invAIriant framework holds together as its own first subject. Boundaries are the strongest dimension: the CLI genuinely serves the audit without performing it, and the primary/secondary/helper story is coherent. Documentation is clean (139/139 links resolve; no jargon leak). No S0 or S1 findings. Two debt items: the tooling does not cross-check that config lens ids exist (INV-001, S2), and the example report JSON cites two finding ids it never defines (INV-002, S3). Verdict: pass, with the two items scheduled.
summary.weakest_reason: the only operability gap of note is that a mistyped mandatory_lens is not caught at validate-config time and fails late during the audit (INV-001)

required_actions.action: Add a config↔lens-library referential-integrity check to validate_framework.py and `invairiant validate-config`.
Reconcile example-report.json with example-audit.md (define CNV-043/044 or stop citing them).
